title: What is Multi-Label Classification?
concept: Multi-label classification assigns zero, one, or multiple independent categories to a single image.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main limitation of single-label classification when analyzing natural scenes?

options.text: It can only predict one class per image, even if multiple exist.
It requires the input images to be in grayscale.

exercises.explain: Correct! Single-label models assume classes are mutually exclusive, which fails when images contain multiple objects.

cards.title: Tagging photos
cards.body: When you upload a vacation photo to social media, you might tag it with #sunset, #beach, and #travel. The photo contains all of these things at once. This is multi-label classification.

cards.title: Multi-label predictions
cards.body: Multi-label classification is a computer vision task where a model predicts the presence or absence of multiple independent classes for a single input image. The presence of one class does not rule out the others.

cards.title: A checklist vs. a ballot
cards.body: Think of single-label classification as a political ballot where you vote for exactly one candidate. Multi-label classification is like a grocery checklist: you can check off milk, eggs, and bread, or none of them, or all of them.

cards.title: Autonomous vehicle perception
cards.body: A self-driving car's forward camera processes a frame and labels it with [pedestrian, traffic_light, car, road]. The model must flag every single class it detects to drive safely.

cards.title: Not just multiple separate models
cards.body: You might think the only way to do this is to train five different models for five labels. In practice, we use a single neural network with multiple output nodes, which is much faster and allows features to be shared.

right.text:
Multi-hot vector: [1, 0, 1, 1]
Means: [Cat, Dog, Bird, Toy]
Values do not need to sum to 1

exercises.prompt: Which of these scenarios is a multi-label classification problem?
exercises.explain: A fashion image can contain a shirt, pants, and shoes simultaneously, requiring multiple independent labels.

options.text: Predicting all clothing items visible in a fashion image
Deciding if an image is a dog or a cat
Finding the exact bounding box of a dog in an image
Predicting the age of a person in years

exercises.prompt: True or false: In multi-label classification, predicting a high probability for 'Car' means the probability for 'Pedestrian' must be low.
exercises.explain: False. Multi-label predictions are independent; a photo can easily contain both a car and a pedestrian.
A multi-hot vector has a 1 at every index corresponding to a present class (e.g., [1, 0, 1]).
exercises.sentence: Multi-label targets are represented using a multi-___ vector of 0s and 1s.

exercises.prompt: Which prediction vector represents a multi-label classification output?
exercises.explain: The first vector has high probabilities for both Cat and Tree independently, which is characteristic of multi-label prediction.

exercises.prompt: You are building an AI photo organizer. For each uploaded photo, the system needs to assign tags like 'Beach', 'Sunny', 'Family', and 'Ocean'. How should you formulate this classification task?

options.text: Multi-label classification, because a single photo can have any combination of these tags.
Single-label classification, because we must choose the single best tag.
Regression, because we want to count the number of pixels in each class.
Object detection, because we need to know the coordinates of the family members.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Since a photo can have multiple tags simultaneously and independently, multi-label classification is the correct formulation. Next, let's see how we activate the model outputs differently for this task!
